channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Adobe is debuting a new web app for creator attribution and protection
item.description: Adobe announced a free web app for creators to apply credentials to their work, in an effort to help protect content from unauthorized use and ensure creators receive proper attribution.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Red Sea cable cuts disrupted up to 70% of traffic, says RETN
item.description: Independent global network services provider RETN says its network diagnostics show that the February 2024 cable cuts in the Red Sea, initially reported to have affected 25% of data traffic between Europe and Asia, caused far more disruption than originally estimated.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: 4 ways your Android phone's Play Store could change soon, if Google loses Epic appeal
item.description: Google lost its legal fight, here's 4 ways your Android phone's Play Store could change soon, if Google loses Epic appeal.
item.dc:creator: hamish.hector@futurenet.com (Hamish Hector), TechRadar

item.title: Lyft drivers will now earn more whenever a ride takes longer than expected
item.description: Lyft drivers will start earning additional pay for extended delays and going out of their way for certain pickups, the company announced Tuesday. The change comes as part of a broader earnings update meant to entice drivers and keep them on the rideshare platform.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Fujifilm X-M5 appears in new leaks and teaser – what to expect from the budget X100VI alternative
item.description: Some new Fujifilm X-M5 leaks suggest the imminent camera will be impressively compact –and pack the company's latest autofocus.
item.dc:creator: mark.wilson@futurenet.com (Mark Wilson), TechRadar

item.title: Breaking Down Data Science Silos with GenAI: A New Playbook for Businesses
item.description: Learn how generative AI (genAI) can help break down data science silos and improve workflows in businesses, enabling faster decision-making and a competitive edge.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Google Forced to Open Play Store to Rival Android App Stores
item.description: Google must open the Play Store to rival third-party Android app stores, a federal judge ruled.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Raspberry Pi Owners Can Now Buy Optimized SD Cards - A Game-Changer for Performance?
item.description: Raspberry Pi owners can now purchase branded SD cards optimized for the Pi ecosystem, promising exceptional performance enhancements and support for command queuing on the Pi 5 specifically.
item.dc:creator: luke.hughes@futurenet.com (Luke Hughes), TechRadar

item.title: How AI is transforming the fan experience
item.description: For billions of people, being a sports fan is about community and identity. Some consider it to be what defines them. It is why they cheer for their teams or wear their jerseys. And it is a lifelong journey, marked by strong loyalty and dedication, making this a unique and interesting cohort.
item.dc:creator: Beshad Bezhadi, Fast Company

item.title: Apple has updated iCloud.com to look shiny and new - here are all the new features you can use right now
item.description: Apple hits refresh on iCloud.com: the site has received both aesthetic and functional updates to enhance user experience.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Apple claims to have found a balance between user privacy and convenience with a tweak in macOS Sequoia: “fewer permission alerts” for screen recording
item.description: MacOS Sequoia 15.1 update will include a tweak that reduces the frequency of permission requests triggered by screen recording apps.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: How IT admins should think about a more open Apple
item.description: Europe’s Digital Markets Act is just one of a series of regulatory actions that are forcing Apple to open up its platforms.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: SAP Build gains AI capabilities to help build autonomous agents
item.description: SAP wants developers to view its Build platform as the one extension solution for all of SAP’s applications, according to Michael Aneling, chief product officer for SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP).
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: States sue TikTok, alleging platform is addictive, harms kids' mental health
item.description: More than a dozen states and the District of Columbia sued TikTok on Tuesday, alleging the platform exploits and harms young users while "deceiving" the public about these dangers.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Musk, Tucker Carlson laugh about post discussing hypothetical Harris assassination
item.description: Conservative media pundit Tucker Carlson hosted tech mogul Elon Musk for an interview on his online video show this week, a sit-down that began with the two laughing about Musk's support for former President Trump and what he says was a "joke" he made about assassination attempts on political figure
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, The Hill

item.title: Wi-Fi 7 brings enterprise-caliber predictability to wireless networks, says Cisco CTO
item.description: The ability to more effectively control wireless networks will go a long way toward making Wi-Fi 7 a viable upgrade for current over-the-air networks and, in some cases, even hard-wired environments. The evolution of Wi-Fi 7 has included more than a decade of spectrum changes and standards updates.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Largest US water utility hit by cyberattack
item.description: The largest water and wastewater utility company in the U.S. was hit by a cyberattack, forcing it to temporarily pause billing for its customers, the company announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Databricks says with its new Databricks Apps platform, you can build tailored enterprise apps in 5 minutes
item.description: Every enterprise today is looking to get as much as possible out of its data and AI investments. But when building in-house apps, developers can struggle with infrastructure constraints and maintenance, security, data governance, compliance, and other issues.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: From risk to reward: Mastering the art of adopting emerging technologies
item.description: One of the greatest things about working in technology is the surprise advancements that take the industry by storm.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: How to activate Google’s newest Android security enhancements
item.description: My fellow Android phone-owners, start your feature-seeking engines: Some significant new security strengtheners on are their way to your device(s) — and quite possibly already there and just waiting to be discovered.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Google Workspace vs. Microsoft 365: What’s the best office suite for business?
item.description: Once upon a time, Microsoft Office ruled the business world. By the late ’90s and early 2000s, Microsoft’s office suite had brushed aside rivals such as WordPerfect Office and Lotus SmartSuite, and there was no competition on the horizon.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Cyber Leaders Exchange: Army, SSA and USCIS cyber chiefs on securing software through automation
item.description: Cybersecurity experts from the Army Software Factory, SSA and USCIS say letting developers drive security efforts drives up safety in code. The post Cyber Leaders Exchange: Army, SSA and USCIS cyber chiefs on securing software through automation first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Australia's online dating industry adopts code of conduct to keep users safer
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — A code of conduct will be enforced on the online dating industry to better protect Australian users after research found that three-in-four people suffer some form of sexual violence through the platforms, Australia’s government said on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Details from New Mexico's lawsuit against Snap show site failed to act on reports of sextortion
item.description: Snapchat failed to act on “rampant” reports of child grooming, sextortion and other dangers to minors on its platform, according to a newly unredacted complaint against the company filed by New Mexico's attorney general. Attorney General Raúl Torrez filed the original complaint on Sept.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: iPhone stuck in SOS mode? Here's how to get out of it
item.description: There are ongoing reports of a widespread outage impacting multiple cellphone and internet providers Monday morning. According to Downdetector.com, AT&T, Verizon and T-Mobile users have all experienced issues.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, NewsNation
